en: probably akin to G2854 (κολλούριον)); a coin-dealer
a cut, i.e. (by analogy) toil (as reducing the strength), literally or figuratively; by implication, pains
to "chop"; specially, to beat the breast in grief
a votive offering and the offering; a consecrated present (to the Temple fund); by extension (the latter term) the Treasury itself, i.e. the room where the contribution boxes stood
orderly arrangement, i.e. decoration; by implication, the world (in a wide or narrow sense, including its inhabitants, literally or figuratively (morally))
properly, to "croak" (as a raven) or scream, i.e. (genitive case) to call aloud (shriek, exclaim, intreat)
a margin, i.e. (specially), a fringe or tassel
to use strength, i.e. seize or retain (literally or figuratively)
kjv: hold (by, fast), keep, lay hand (hold) on, obtain, retain, take (by)

en: a decision (the function or the effect, for or against ("crime"))
kjv: avenge, condemned, condemnation, damnation, + go to law, judgment

en: original formation (properly, the act; by implication, the thing, literally or figuratively)
akin to G2947 (κυλιόω)); i.e. in a circle (by implication, of G1722 (ἐν)), i.e. (adverbially) all around
compare G2945 (κύκλῳ), G1507 (εἱλίσσω)); to roll about
rocking about, i.e. crippled (maimed, in feet or hands)
i.e. Cyrenæan, i.e. inhabitant of Cyrene
supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
to estop, i.e. prevent (by word or act)
kjv: forbid, hinder, keep from, let, not suffer, withstand

en: blunted, i.e. (figuratively) of hearing (deaf) or speech (dumb)

kjv: preach, say, speak (after), talk, tell, utter

en: while G138 (αἱρέομαι) is more violent, to seize or remove))
kjv: accept, + be amazed, assay, attain, bring, X when I call, catch, come on (X unto), + forget, have, hold, obtain, receive (X after), take (away, up)

en: to lie hid (literally or figuratively); often used adverbially, unwittingly
kjv: be hid, be ignorant of, unawares

en: a people (in general; thus differing from G1218 (δῆμος), which denotes one's own populace)

properly, to "lay" forth, i.e. (figuratively) relate (in words (usually of systematic or set discourse; whereas G2036 (ἔπω) and G5346 (φημί) generally refer to an individual expression or speech respectively; while G4483 (ῥέω) is properly to break silence merely, and G2980 (λαλέω) means an extended or random harangue)); by implication, to mean
kjv: ask, bid, boast, call, describe, give out, name, put forth, say(-ing, on), shew, speak, tell, utter

en: something scaled (light), i.e. a small coin

something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expression (i.e. Christ)
kjv: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech, talk, thing, + none of these things move me, tidings, treatise, utterance, word, work
other, which remain, remnant, residue, rest
besides, finally, furthermore, (from) henceforth, moreover, now, + it remaineth, then

en: to distress; reflexively or passively, to be sad
kjv: cause grief, grieve, be in heaviness, (be) sorrow(-ful), be (make) sorry

en: something to loosen with, i.e. a redemption price (figuratively, atonement)
a portable lamp or other illuminator (literally or figuratively)

kjv: break (up), destroy, dissolve, (un-)loose, melt, put off

en: a female Magdalene, i.e. inhabitant of Magdala
(adverbially) more (in a greater degree)) or rather
kjv: + better, X far, (the) more (and more), (so) much (the more), rather

en: Maria or Mariam (i.e. Mirjam), the name of six Christian females
something evidential, i.e. (genitive case) evidence given or (specially), the Decalogue (in the sacred Tabernacle)
a witness (literally (judicially) or figuratively (genitive case)); by analogy, a "martyr"
a whip (literally, the Roman flagellum for criminals; figuratively, a disease)
Matthæus (i.e. Matthitjah), an Israelite and a Christian
a knife, i.e. dirk; figuratively, war, judicial punishment
big (literally or figuratively, in a very wide application)
kjv: (+ fear) exceedingly, great(-est), high, large, loud, mighty, + (be) sore (afraid), strong, X to years
